subject: Public Health
title: Cholera Pandemics of the 1800s
description: A quick win: understand Cholera Pandemics of the 1800s.

hook.text: Cholera escaped India in 1817 and circled the globe six times over the next century.

buildup.text: Contaminated water spread the bacterium Vibrio cholerae through growing industrial cities.

discovery.text: Cholera killed so fast—dehydration within hours—that people collapsed in the streets.

twist.text: Cholera forced cities to build modern sewer systems—the outbreaks drove sanitation revolutions.

climax.text: London's 'Great Stink' of 1858 finally pushed Parliament to fund Joseph Bazalgette's sewer network.

punchline.text: Cholera didn't just kill people. It built modern cities.

quiz.question: What major infrastructure did cholera outbreaks drive cities to build?
